name: Castillo de Andaluz y Iglesia de Nuestra Señora – circular por el Camino del Cid desde Castilllejo de Robledo
region_intro: Hiking around Castillejo De Robledo offers diverse natural landscapes characterized by striking white rock formations and extensive forests of juniper and holm oak. The region features a valley carved by the Arroyo de la Nava, alongside expansive limestone moors and the fertile banks of the Duero River. Hikers can explore a terrain that combines scenic beauty with historical sites, including a ruined Templar castle and ancient Romanesque churches. Best hiking trails around Castillejo De Robledo The most popular hiking route is Pantano de Linares y Maderuelo — circular, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) easy trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168) — circular desde Vadocondes, a moderate 11.4 miles (18.4 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including valleys and woodlands. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Andaluz y Iglesia de Nuestra Señora – circular por el Camino del Cid desde Castilllejo de Robledo, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Castillejo De Robledo is defined by white rock formations, juniper and holm oak forests, and limestone moors. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Castillejo De Robledo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Castillejo De Robledo's varied terrain.
tips: The Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Castillejo de Robledo is a 12th-century Romanesque temple, frequently attributed to the Templars although without definitive proof.

Castillejo de Robledo Castle is a medieval fortress located in the town of Castillejo de Robledo. This castle has a rich history, as it was built on an old Muslim fortification in the 12th century and later became the property of the Knights Templar.
The castle is notable for its double walled enclosure and its towers, including an irregularly shaped keep and a pentagonal tower that rises above a cliff. Although it is currently in ruins, it remains a site of great historical and architectural interest.
Along this route, you'll find numerous fields and landscapes reminiscent of medieval times and the exploits of El Cid Campeador.
